content.text:
{
  "tests": [
    {
      "filename": "001_nonexistent_source_fails.sh",
      "body": "#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

tmpdir=$(mktemp -d)
trap 'rm -rf "$tmpdir"' EXIT

set +e
"$UTIL" "$tmpdir/no_such_source" "$tmpdir/dest" >/dev/null 2>&1
status=$?
set -e

if [[ $status -ne 0 ]]; then
  exit 0
else
  echo "expected nonexistent source to fail" >&2
  exit 1
fi
",
      "exercises": "Nonexistent SOURCE: DESCRIPTION says "Rename SOURCE to DEST, or move SOURCE(s) to DIRECTORY."",
      "expected": "The utility exits with a nonzero status.",
      "expected_to_fail": true
    },
    {
      "filename": "002_no_operands_fails.sh",
      "body": "#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

tmpdir=$(mktemp -d)
trap 'rm -rf "$tmpdir"' EXIT

set +e
"$UTIL" >/dev/null 2>&1
status=$?
set -e

if [[ $status -ne 0 ]]; then
  exit 0
else
  echo "expected no operands to fail" >&2
  exit 1
fi
",
      "exercises": "Missing required operands: SYNOPSIS documents only forms requiring SOURCE DEST, SOURCE... DIRECTORY, or -t DIRECTORY SOURCE...",
      "expected": "The utility exits with a nonzero status.",
      "expected_to_fail": true
    },
    {
      "filename": "003_single_operand_missing_dest_fails.sh",
      "body": "#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

tmpdir=$(mktemp -d)
trap 'rm -rf "$tmpdir"' EXIT
printf 'data' > "$tmpdir/src"

set +e
"$UTIL" "$tmpdir/src" >/dev/null 2>&1
status=$?
set -e

if [[ $status -ne 0 ]]; then
  exit 0
else
  echo "expected missing destination to fail" >&2
  exit 1
fi
",
      "exercises": "Missing DEST/DIRECTORY: SYNOPSIS documents no one-operand form; it requires SOURCE DEST, SOURCE... DIRECTORY, or -t DIRECTORY SOURCE...",
      "expected": "The utility exits with a nonzero status.",
      "expected_to_fail": true
    },
    {
      "filename": "004_short_t_missing_argument_fails.sh",
      "body": "#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

tmpdir=$(mktemp -d)
trap 'rm -rf "$tmpdir"' EXIT

set +e
"$UTIL" -t >/dev/null 2>&1
status=$?
set -e

if [[ $status -ne 0 ]]; then
  exit 0
else
  echo "expected -t without argument to fail" >&2
  exit 1
fi
",
      "exercises": "-t requires DIRECTORY: manpage says "-t, --target-directory=DIRECTORY move all SOURCE arguments into DIRECTORY" and "Mandatory arguments to long options are mandatory for short options too."",
      "expected": "The utility exits with a nonzero status.",
      "expected_to_fail": true
    },
    {
      "filename": "005_long_target_directory_missing_argument_fails.sh",
      "body": "#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

tmpdir=$(mktemp -d)
trap 'rm -rf "$tmpdir"' EXIT

set +e
"$UTIL" --target-directory >/dev/null 2>&1
status=$?
set -e

if [[ $status -ne 0 ]]; then
  exit 0
else
  echo "expected --target-directory without argument to fail" >&2
  exit 1
fi
",
      "exercises": "--target-directory requires DIRECTORY: manpage says "-t, --target-directory=DIRECTORY move all SOURCE arguments into DIRECTORY."",
      "expected": "The utility exits with a nonzero status.",
      "expected_to_fail": true
    },
    {
      "filename": "006_t_nonexistent_directory_fails.sh",
      "body": "#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

tmpdir=$(mktemp -d)
trap 'rm -rf "$tmpdir"' EXIT
printf 'data' > "$tmpdir/src"

set +e
"$UTIL" -t "$tmpdir/no_such_directory" "$tmpdir/src" >/dev/null 2>&1
status=$?
set -e

if [[ $status -ne 0 ]]; then
  exit 0
else
  echo "expected nonexistent target directory to fail" >&2
  exit 1
fi
",
      "exercises": "-t DIRECTORY requires an actual directory: manpage says "-t, --target-directory=DIRECTORY move all SOURCE arguments into DIRECTORY."",
      "expected": "The utility exits with a nonzero status.",
      "expected_to_fail": true
    },
    {
      "filename": "007_T_existing_directory_target_fails.sh",
      "body": "#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

tmpdir=$(mktemp -d)
trap 'rm -rf "$tmpdir"' EXIT
printf 'data' > "$tmpdir/src"
mkdir "$tmpdir/destdir"

set +e
"$UTIL" -T "$tmpdir/src" "$tmpdir/destdir" >/dev/null 2>&1
status=$?
set -e

if [[ $status -ne 0 ]]; then
  exit 0
else
  echo "expected -T with directory target to fail" >&2
  exit 1
fi
",
      "exercises": "-T with directory target: manpage says "-T, --no-target-directory treat DEST as a normal file."",
      "expected": "The utility exits with a nonzero status.",
      "expected_to_fail": true
    },
    {
      "filename": "008_update_none_fail_existing_dest_fails.sh",
      "body": "#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

tmpdir=$(mktemp -d)
trap 'rm -rf "$tmpdir"' EXIT
printf 'new' > "$tmpdir/src"
printf 'old' > "$tmpdir/dst"

set +e
"$UTIL" --update=none-fail "$tmpdir/src" "$tmpdir/dst" >/dev/null 2>&1
status=$?
set -e

if [[ $status -ne 0 ]]; then
  exit 0
else
  echo "expected --update=none-fail skip to fail" >&2
  exit 1
fi
",
      "exercises": "--update=none-fail: manpage says skipped files "are diagnosed and induce a failure."",
      "expected": "The utility exits with a nonzero status.",
      "expected_to_fail": true
    },
    {
      "filename": "009_update_empty_value_fails.sh",
      "body": "#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

tmpdir=$(mktemp -d)
trap 'rm -rf "$tmpdir"' EXIT
printf 'new' > "$tmpdir/src"
printf 'old' > "$tmpdir/dst"

set +e
"$UTIL" --update= "$tmpdir/src" "$tmpdir/dst" >/dev/null 2>&1
status=$?
set -e

if [[ $status -ne 0 ]]; then
  exit 0
else
  echo "expected empty --update value to fail" >&2
  exit 1
fi
",
      "exercises": "Invalid empty UPDATE value: manpage restricts UPDATE to "{all,none,none-fail,older(default)}".",
      "expected": "The utility exits with a nonzero status.",
      "expected_to_fail": true
    },
    {
      "filename": "010_update_none_skips_without_failure.sh",
      "body": "#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

tmpdir=$(mktemp -d)
trap 'rm -rf "$tmpdir"' EXIT
printf 'new' > "$tmpdir/src"
printf 'old' > "$tmpdir/dst"

"$UTIL" --update=none "$tmpdir/src" "$tmpdir/dst"
content="$(cat "$tmpdir/dst" 2>/dev/null || true)"

if [[ $content == "old" ]]; then
  exit 0
else
  echo "expected --update=none to leave destination unchanged" >&2
  exit 1
fi
",
      "exercises": "--update=none: documented as like --no-clobber, with no destination files replaced and skipped files not inducing failure.",
      "expected": "The destination file keeps its original contents.",
      "expected_to_fail": false
    },
    {
      "filename": "011_update_all_replaces_existing.sh",
      "body": "#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

tmpdir=$(mktemp -d)
trap 'rm -rf "$tmpdir"' EXIT
printf 'new' > "$tmpdir/src"
printf 'old' > "$tmpdir/dst"

"$UTIL" --update=all "$tmpdir/src" "$tmpdir/dst"
content="$(cat "$tmpdir/dst" 2>/dev/null || true)"

if [[ $content == "new" ]]; then
  exit 0
else
  echo "expected --update=all to replace destination" >&2
  exit 1
fi
",
      "exercises": "--update=all: documented as resulting in all existing files in the destination being replaced.",
      "expected": "The destination file contains the source contents.",
      "expected_to_fail": false
    },
    {
      "filename": "012_no_clobber_skips_existing.sh",
      "body": "#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

tmpdir=$(mktemp -d)
trap 'rm -rf "$tmpdir"' EXIT
printf 'new' > "$tmpdir/src"
printf 'old' > "$tmpdir/dst"

"$UTIL" -n "$tmpdir/src" "$tmpdir/dst"
content="$(cat "$tmpdir/dst" 2>/dev/null || true)"

if [[ $content == "old" ]]; then
  exit 0
else
  echo "expected -n to leave destination unchanged" >&2
  exit 1
fi
",
      "exercises": "-n/--no-clobber: documented to not overwrite an existing file.",
      "expected": "The destination file keeps its original contents.",
      "expected_to_fail": false
    },
    {
      "filename": "013_final_n_then_f_overwrites.sh",
      "body": "#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

tmpdir=$(mktemp -d)
trap 'rm -rf "$tmpdir"' EXIT
printf 'new' > "$tmpdir/src"
printf 'old' > "$tmpdir/dst"

"$UTIL" -n -f "$tmpdir/src" "$tmpdir/dst"
content="$(cat "$tmpdir/dst" 2>/dev/null || true)"

if [[ $content == "new" ]]; then
  exit 0
else
  echo "expected final -f to override -n" >&2
  exit 1
fi
",
      "exercises": "Final overwrite option wins: manpage says if more than one of -i, -f, -n is specified, only the final one takes effect.",
      "expected": "The destination file is overwritten because -f is final.",
      "expected_to_fail": false
    },
    {
      "filename": "014_final_f_then_n_skips.sh",
      "body": "#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

tmpdir=$(mktemp -d)
trap 'rm -rf "$tmpdir"' EXIT
printf 'new' > "$tmpdir/src"
printf 'old' > "$tmpdir/dst"

"$UTIL" -f -n "$tmpdir/src" "$tmpdir/dst"
content="$(cat "$tmpdir/dst" 2>/dev/null || true)"

if [[ $content == "old" ]]; then
  exit 0
else
  echo "expected final -n to override -f" >&2
  exit 1
fi
",
      "exercises": "Final overwrite option wins: manpage says if more than one of -i, -f, -n is specified, only the final one takes effect.",
      "expected": "The destination file is not overwritten because -n is final.",
      "expected_to_fail": false
    }
  ]
}
